textPlain: I tried to search their wiki for wayland, but got zero hits so I guess this is for X11 only?Edit: nevermind, I see this is relatively "ancient" software. Last release over 10 years ago :)Will take it for a spin on an old computer though

>  EDE (Equinox Desktop Environment) is a simple and fast desktop environment for *nix-like systems. It uses the FLTK GUI toolkit and offers a familiar look and feel. EDE is designed according to the UNIX philosophy, which means it offers seperate executables for each component to “do one job and do it good”. This makes EDE very modular and also easy to alter for any user's special needs and requirements.TIHI, but I can definitely see places where this would be useful. Based on the name, I thought it was just Enlightenment/E rebrand or something.
Wow! This looks like XP/Win2k. Gotta put this on the parents computer, they'll be happy they got their old Windows back. (They don't really like Xfce)
Very cool! Love the crystal icon set. I remember when that was first released, it was mind blowing.
Funny this should be posted. I was just considering this for a Boot CD project a few months ago.
I like the look of this. It looks like mate, which I presently use. I wonder what the difference is
Any chance of an rPi image?

Have we arrived at where everybody knows what a desktop environment should look like?I mean, an end to experiments and variants and improvements. We all know how it should be and anything else is wrong.I would like that.
The Q4OS distro has an addon called XPQ4 which has some old Windows themes https://xpq4.sourceforge.io/
Maybe they like LXQt more.XFCE can be themed to be almost identical to XP.
i imagine it’s trivial to install on raspian?
